title: Token panel: cost/rate sparkline using existing token_events history
description: internal/metrics/cache.go already persists every token event in the token_events SQLite table (used for QueryTokensHybrid). Add a query that buckets the last N intervals (e.g. last 30 min in 1-min buckets) of cost or tokens/min, and render it as a compact sparkline (unicode block chars, no new dependency) in the token panel in internal/ui/dashboard.go next to the current rate figure. Makes usage trend visible at a glance instead of only the instantaneous rate.

title: Migrate CI to Argo Workflows
description: Replace any existing GitHub Actions CI with an Argo Workflows WorkflowTemplate in iad-ci. Template should: go fmt check, go vet, go build. ccdash is a Go project. Store template in jedarden/declarative-config under k8s/iad-ci/argo-workflows/ccdash-ci.yaml. Follow patterns from existing WorkflowTemplates in that repo.

title: Multi-directory JSONL scanning
description: Allow ccdash to aggregate token usage from JSONL files across multiple directories rather than a single path. Configurable via CLI flag or config file. Should support glob patterns or a list of directories. Useful for users with multiple Claude Code project roots.

title: Add ccdash --test-notify flag to debug notifier webhook delivery
description: Follow-up to ADR-0006 (see docs/plan.md, section 'ADR-0006', and its mirrored copy under docs/adrs/, ADR number 0006). Once the notifier lands, a misconfigured webhook_url fails silently by design (see ADR consequences), which makes it undebuggable. Add 'ccdash --test-notify' that loads ~/.ccdash/config.yaml, sends one synthetic test payload to notify.webhook_url, and prints success/failure with the HTTP status/error to stdout — a one-shot CLI check, no TUI involved. Blocked on the notifier bead landing first.

title: Add headless ccdash --once/--json snapshot flag
description: ccdash is only consumable interactively today (Bubble Tea TUI). Add 'ccdash --once --json' that runs one collection cycle (system/token/session metrics, respecting the same collectors already in internal/metrics/) and prints a single JSON snapshot to stdout instead of starting the TUI loop, then exits. Enables scripting and external consumption without a terminal (e.g. FABRIC, the lab NEEDLE-telemetry dashboard, could poll this instead of re-implementing tmux/hook parsing; or ad-hoc curl-free health checks in CI). cmd/ccdash/main.go already parses flags for --install-hooks etc., follow that pattern.

title: Token panel: configurable cost-threshold color alert
description: Add a configurable spend threshold (extends the ~/.ccdash/config.yaml introduced for ADR-0006's notifier, e.g. alerts.cost_threshold_usd) that changes the token panel's total-cost display to a warning color once the current lookback window's spend crosses it. Purely local/visual, no network dependency even though it shares the config file with the notifier feature. Implement in internal/metrics/tokens.go (TokenMetrics already computes TotalCost) and internal/ui/dashboard.go (token panel render). Reasonable default: disabled/unset (no threshold) so existing users see no behavior change.

title: Implement ADR-0006: leader-only debounced push notification on session escalation
description: Implement the notifier decided in ADR-0006 (docs/plan.md, section 'ADR-0006'; full copy in docs/adrs/, ADR number 0006). Add ~/.ccdash/config.yaml (notify.enabled bool default false, notify.webhook_url string) and a new internal/notify/ package. In the existing refresh loop, only the lease-holding leader (internal/metrics/cache.go TryAcquireLease) diffs prior vs current HookSession status per session; on a transition into waiting/asking that persists >~15s, POST a compact JSON payload (session name, project dir, idle duration only — no token/cost data) to notify.webhook_url. Silent-fail (one-line log warning) on unreachable/misconfigured webhook, never crash the dashboard. Fully inert with zero network calls when notify.enabled is false (the default).

title: Auto-check-for-update banner on startup (currently manual-only via 'u')
description: internal/updater/updater.go (CheckForUpdate) is only invoked when the user presses 'u'. Given ccdash ships real bug fixes across point releases (e.g. 1.0.2 fixed sessions stuck WORKING when actually blocked on human input — a correctness bug, not cosmetic), users on stale binaries silently keep the old broken behavior. On startup, do one non-blocking CheckForUpdate call and show a small dismissible banner/status-bar note if a newer version exists (no auto-install, matches existing opt-in self-update UX). Keep it non-blocking so offline/no-network startup is unaffected (mirror existing error handling in CheckForUpdate).

title: Publish ccdash binary as GitHub Release via Argo CI
description: On successful CI pass, have Argo Workflows publish the ccdash binary as a GitHub Release artifact. Tag-triggered. Build multi-arch binaries (linux-amd64, linux-arm64, darwin-amd64, darwin-arm64). Follow the pattern from forge-ci or needle-ci WorkflowTemplates in jedarden/declarative-config. Include SHA256 checksums.

title: Session panel: flag/sort sessions idle in READY/waiting state past a threshold
description: internal/ui/dashboard.go session panel currently shows READY/waiting sessions with no indication of how long they've been waiting. Add a staleness threshold (e.g. 5m) past which a waiting session is visually flagged (bold/distinct color) and/or sorted to the top of its group, so a user scanning a 10-15+ session fleet (this host runs 15 concurrent tmux sessions as of 2026-07-20) can spot the one stuck the longest without needing ADR-0006's push notifications. Standalone feature, no dependency on the notifier work. Source the idle duration from HookSession.LastActivity / LastStop already tracked in internal/metrics/hooks.go.

title: Network I/O monitoring panel
description: Add a network I/O panel to ccdash showing bandwidth usage per process (bytes sent/received per second), complementing the existing CPU/memory panels. Use /proc/net/dev or per-process /proc/<pid>/net/dev on Linux. Display as a scrollable table similar to existing panels.

title: Add ccdash --export csv|json to dump the SQLite token cache for external analysis
description: internal/metrics/cache.go's SQLite token_events table already holds full per-event history but is only ever queried in aggregate for the live TUI. Add 'ccdash --export csv' / 'ccdash --export json' that dumps the raw or lightly-aggregated event history to stdout for external analysis (e.g. feeding the vibe-coding-discovery AI-coding-analytics project, or ad-hoc spreadsheet work). Read-only dump of existing local data, no schema changes needed.
